title: Reginald
author: Saki
description: The debut collection introducing Saki's razor-witted aristocratic idler Reginald, whose languid epigrams and social mischief skewered Edwardian drawing-room conventions with devastating precision.

title: The Chronicles of Clovis
author: Saki
description: A sparkling collection of stories featuring the amoral young wit Clovis Sangrail, whose audacious pranks and supernatural tales expose the absurdities lurking beneath Edwardian society's polished surface.

title: The Unbearable Bassington
author: Saki
description: Saki's only completed novel follows the charming, irredeemably selfish Comus Bassington as his mother's social ambitions and his own reckless nature propel him toward an inevitable colonial exile.

title: Beasts and Super-Beasts
author: Saki
description: Saki's finest collection, where animals exact revenge on imperious humans, supernatural horror lurks in country houses, and darkly comic reversals punctuate stories that remain his most widely anthologized work.

title: Dusty Answer
author: Rosamond Lehmann
description: A young woman's intense, ambiguous attachments to her glamorous Cambridge neighbors unfold over years with devastating emotional clarity, establishing Lehmann as a bold new voice in the literature of desire.

title: Invitation to the Waltz
author: Rosamond Lehmann
description: A teenage girl prepares for her first formal dance in this acutely observed portrait of adolescent consciousness, capturing social anxiety and nascent desire with Lehmann's characteristic lyrical precision.

title: The Weather in the Streets
author: Rosamond Lehmann
description: The sequel to Invitation to the Waltz follows Olivia into an adulterous affair with a married man, tracing the emotional and physical costs of passion with painful frankness rare for its era.

title: The Ballad and the Source
author: Rosamond Lehmann
description: A young girl gradually uncovers the tragic history of the magnetic, formidable Mrs. Jardine through layered testimonies, creating a modernist portrait of female power, loss, and mythic self-invention.

title: The Echoing Grove
author: Rosamond Lehmann
description: Two sisters whose lives have been entwined and shattered by love for the same man finally confront their shared history in Lehmann's most psychologically intricate and emotionally devastating novel.

title: The Great Fortune
author: Olivia Manning
description: The opening volume of the Balkan Trilogy follows a British couple newly arrived in Bucharest on the eve of World War II, capturing a civilization's last brilliant moments before the catastrophe descends.

title: The Spoilt City
author: Olivia Manning
description: As Romania falls under German influence and Bucharest grows hostile, Guy and Harriet Pringle struggle to survive amid betrayal and political terror in the second volume of Manning's Balkan Trilogy.

title: Friends and Heroes
author: Olivia Manning
description: The Pringles flee to Athens as Greece faces the Axis invasion, and Harriet's marriage strains under wartime dislocation in the luminous final volume of Manning's Balkan Trilogy.

title: The Danger Tree
author: Olivia Manning
description: Manning's Levant Trilogy begins as Harriet Pringle arrives in Cairo, finding the city electric with rumor as Rommel advances and wartime society spins in desperate, glittering uncertainty.

title: The Battle Lost and Won
author: Olivia Manning
description: The second Levant volume follows the desert campaign to El Alamein, weaving personal and historical loss into a devastating portrait of men and women consumed by war's machinery.

title: The Sum of Things
author: Olivia Manning
description: The final Levant volume resolves the fate of Harriet and Guy Pringle as the war turns in North Africa, completing one of the most ambitious chronicles of World War II in English fiction.

title: The Shrimp and the Anemone
author: L.P. Hartley
description: The first of the Eustace and Hilda trilogy traces the childhood of a sensitive boy dominated by his intense, self-sacrificing sister, establishing the psychic bonds that will shape their entire lives.

title: The Sixth Heaven
author: L.P. Hartley
description: Eustace Cherrington, now at Oxford, navigates postwar social change while his sister Hilda's life begins its fateful trajectory in the subtly tense second volume of Hartley's celebrated trilogy.

title: Eustace and Hilda
author: L.P. Hartley
description: The concluding volume of Hartley's trilogy brings Eustace to Venice and to the devastating resolution of his sister's life, rendering the psychology of guilt and devotion with haunting exactitude.

title: The Go-Between
author: L.P. Hartley
description: A twelve-year-old boy carries secret letters between a tenant farmer and a young aristocratic woman during one sweltering Edwardian summer, an innocence-destroying experience he has never recovered from by old age.

title: The Hireling
author: L.P. Hartley
description: A war-widowed lady and her chauffeur develop a delusional attachment across Britain's rigid class divide, building to a violent rupture that lays bare the cruelties of social distance.

title: A Town Like Alice
author: Nevil Shute
description: A young English woman survives a brutal wartime death march through Malaya and then devotes her life and inherited fortune to transforming a desolate Australian outback settlement into a thriving town.

title: On the Beach
author: Nevil Shute
description: In the last habitable corner of Earth after nuclear war, an American submarine commander and Melbourne's remaining civilians wait for the radioactive cloud that will end all human life.

title: Pied Piper
author: Nevil Shute
description: An elderly English widower attempts a quiet fishing holiday in France only to find himself shepherding an ever-growing group of refugee children across Nazi-occupied Europe toward the coast.

title: The Chequer Board
author: Nevil Shute
description: A terminally ill man retraces the lives of three men he briefly met in a wartime hospital, uncovering stories of race, dignity, and human connection that reframe his own dwindling life.

title: Trustee from the Toolroom
author: Nevil Shute
description: A modest London hobbyist with no money or connections crosses the world on a series of improvised voyages to recover a hidden fortune for his orphaned niece, driven purely by quiet, unbreakable integrity.

title: No Highway
author: Nevil Shute
description: An eccentric aeronautical scientist becomes convinced his calculations prove the aircraft he is flying will suffer catastrophic metal fatigue — and is forced to act on his own unverified theory mid-flight.

title: Round the Bend
author: Nevil Shute
description: An aircraft engineer narrates the life of his friend Tom Cutter, a half-Eurasian mechanic who develops a quasi-religious philosophy of craftsmanship that draws a devoted following across Asia.

title: The Sketch Book of Geoffrey Crayon
author: Washington Irving
description: Irving's landmark miscellany introduced 'Rip Van Winkle' and 'The Legend of Sleepy Hollow' to the world, and effectively invented the American short story as a literary form.

title: Bracebridge Hall
author: Washington Irving
description: A genial follow-up to the Sketch Book centered on an eccentric English country squire's household, blending gentle social comedy, ghost stories, and romantic sketches in Irving's warmly nostalgic style.

title: Tales of a Traveller
author: Washington Irving
description: A collection of supernatural tales, comic sketches, and robber-band stories narrated by a touring Englishman, showcasing Irving's range from gothic horror to satirical burlesque.

title: The Deerslayer
author: James Fenimore Cooper
description: The first adventure of young Natty Bumppo, the ideal frontiersman, as he navigates his first conflict with the Huron on the shores of Lake Otsego, laying out Cooper's mythology of the American wilderness.

title: The Last of the Mohicans
author: James Fenimore Cooper
description: Hawkeye and the last Mohican warriors guide two colonial women through the wilderness during the French and Indian War, in the most celebrated and widely read of Cooper's Leatherstocking Tales.

title: The Pathfinder
author: James Fenimore Cooper
description: Natty Bumppo guides a small party through the Great Lakes frontier during the French and Indian War and briefly pursues a romantic life he ultimately knows he is unfit for.

title: The Pioneers
author: James Fenimore Cooper
description: An aging Natty Bumppo clashes with the encroaching laws of a frontier settlement, and Cooper mourns the vanishing wilderness in the novel that launched one of American literature's great saga sequences.

title: The Prairie
author: James Fenimore Cooper
description: The aged Natty Bumppo spends his final years on the vast western prairie, offering a majestic and elegiac farewell to Cooper's iconic frontiersman and to the wild America he embodied.

title: The Rise of Silas Lapham
author: William Dean Howells
description: A self-made Boston paint manufacturer navigates the treacherous social rituals of Gilded Age society and faces a defining moral crisis in business, in Howells's masterpiece of American realism.

title: A Modern Instance
author: William Dean Howells
description: An ambitious journalist and his proud wife drift apart in the first American novel to treat divorce as a serious literary subject, with unflinching psychological realism that scandalized contemporary readers.

title: Indian Summer
author: William Dean Howells
description: A middle-aged American journalist returning to Florence after twenty years becomes entangled in a romantic triangle involving a charming widow and a much younger woman, in Howells's most elegant comedy of manners.

title: A Hazard of New Fortunes
author: William Dean Howells
description: A magazine editor relocates his family to New York and encounters the full spectrum of Gilded Age life — labor strikes, socialism, and immigrant poverty — in Howells's most ambitious social novel.

title: The Landlord at Lion's Head
author: William Dean Howells
description: A New England farmhouse is transformed into a fashionable summer resort by an ambitious young man whose moral coarseness Howells dissects with characteristic precision and ironic restraint.

title: McTeague
author: Frank Norris
description: A brutish, unlicensed San Francisco dentist and his miserly wife descend into violence and ruin, in Norris's savage naturalist study of greed and hereditary determinism inspired by Zola.

title: The Octopus
author: Frank Norris
description: California wheat ranchers wage a desperate struggle against the Southern Pacific Railroad's stranglehold on their land and livelihoods in the epic first volume of Norris's unfinished Epic of the Wheat trilogy.

title: The Pit
author: Frank Norris
description: A Chicago speculator attempts to corner the wheat market in the frenzied trading pits of the Board of Trade, while his marriage quietly collapses in the second volume of Norris's wheat trilogy.

title: Vandover and the Brute
author: Frank Norris
description: A talented San Francisco artist written before McTeague but published posthumously, tracing one man's surrender to his bestial inner nature through drink and moral weakness in unsparing naturalist detail.

title: Blix
author: Frank Norris
description: A semi-autobiographical romance set in San Francisco follows a young journalist and a strong-willed woman who form an unconventional companionship that frees him to pursue serious literary ambitions.

title: them
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: A multigenerational Detroit family fights poverty, violence, and social upheaval across thirty years, culminating in the 1967 riots, in the National Book Award-winning novel that established Oates's epic ambition.

title: A Garden of Earthly Delights
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: The first of Oates's American trilogy follows a migrant worker's daughter across three generations as she claws toward security and respectability in a violent, abundantly indifferent American landscape.

title: Expensive People
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: A morbidly obese suburban boy confesses to murdering his beautiful, coldly ambitious mother in this darkly satirical second volume of Oates's American trilogy, dissecting the spiritual vacancy of affluence.

title: Wonderland
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: A boy who survives his father's murder-suicide rampage spends a lifetime searching for a stable self through medicine and family, in an expansive and unsettling portrait of American identity and violence.

title: Do with Me What You Will
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: A beautiful, eerily passive woman and the idealistic lawyer who falls for her intersect at a Detroit criminal trial, exploring passion, will, and the nature of moral choice in Oates's richest middle-period novel.

title: Bellefleur
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: A multigenerational saga of a monstrous upstate New York dynasty told with gothic extravagance and supernatural intensity, the most formally ambitious of Oates's experimental novel-cycles.

title: Because It Is Bitter, and Because It Is My Heart
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: The friendship between a white girl from a declining family and a Black man who kills to protect her is slowly sundered by race in small-town 1950s New York, with devastating historical and moral weight.

title: Black Water
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: A young woman drowns in a submerged car while a powerful senator swims free in this harrowing, stream-of-consciousness reckoning with the Chappaquiddick incident and the expendability of women.

title: Foxfire: Confessions of a Girl Gang
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: Five working-class girls in 1950s upstate New York form a secret gang to fight back against male predation and poverty, building toward a radical communal experiment narrated decades later with fierce nostalgia.

title: What I Lived For
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: A driven, self-destructive Syracuse businessman tears through a Memorial Day weekend confronting his father's unsolved murder and his own compromised life in an exhausting, virtuosic portrait of masculine crisis.

title: Zombie
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: A sex offender on parole chronicles his compulsion to create a zombie slave in a chillingly matter-of-fact first-person voice, a formally daring and deeply disturbing study in predatory psychopathology.

title: We Were the Mulvaneys
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: A prosperous upstate New York family slowly disintegrates after their daughter is raped, tracing the unspeakable damage done by shame, silence, and masculine pride across two decades.

title: Blonde
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: A biographical novel imagines Marilyn Monroe's interior life from childhood trauma to Hollywood stardom, treating her as a mythic American figure whose destruction was written into the culture that created her.

title: The Falls
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: A marriage begins at Niagara Falls with a suicide and ends in another catastrophe decades later, as Oates weaves the Love Canal environmental disaster into a multigenerational American tragedy.

title: The Gravedigger's Daughter
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: A young woman flees her immigrant family's violent destruction in 1930s upstate New York and reinvents herself across America over decades, in a sweeping saga of survival, identity, and erasure.

title: A Widow's Story
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: A raw, unflinching memoir of the sudden death of Oates's husband of forty-seven years and the months of grief, bewilderment, and suicidal despair that followed — one of the great modern memoirs of mourning.

title: Mudwoman
author: Joyce Carol Oates
description: A university president who survived abandonment in a swamp as a toddler watches her constructed identity fracture under professional and personal crisis in one of Oates's darkest psychological portraits.
